content.verdict: One&Only Nyungwe House is the southern-Rwanda complement to the gorilla loop — 22 rooms set inside the Gisakura tea estate, surrounded on three sides by Nyungwe National Park's Afromontane rainforest. Chimp trekking, the famous canopy walkway, 13 primate species, and 300+ bird species are the draws. Hot pool, spa with fynbos rituals, and the tea-estate setting make this an ideal pre/post gorilla decompression.
content.best_room: Forest View suites (75 m²) face directly into the rainforest canopy — wake to chimp calls. The 1 Suite (the master suite) has a private outdoor cedar tub on the terrace for two. The Tea Plantation View rooms are slightly more affordable with sweeping green views.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Kigali to Nyungwe
itinerary_7_nights.description: 5h scenic drive from Kigali via Butare and Huye (lunch at Hotel Faucon). Arrival at Nyungwe House late afternoon. Welcome ritual with Rwandan tea from the surrounding estate. Settle into your suite. Dinner of Nile perch and tea-smoked beef.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Chimpanzee trek
itinerary_7_nights.description: 5am breakfast, 6am check-in at Uwinka park HQ. Chimpanzee trek (1–4h depending on the troop's location). Less guaranteed than gorillas (~70% success rate). Return for late lunch by the pool. Couples' Africology rainforest spa ritual in the afternoon.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Canopy walk + tea estate tour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Famous Igishigishigi canopy walkway — 60m above the rainforest floor, 90m long. Mid-morning tour of the surrounding tea estate including a hand-picking session and tasting at the One&Only Tea Bar. Afternoon at the heated pool. Sundowner aperitif.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Colobus monkey trek + Kamiranzovu waterfall
itinerary_7_nights.description: Easier 2h trek tracking a 400-strong troop of black-and-white colobus monkeys. Picnic lunch by the Kamiranzovu marsh. Afternoon spa massage. Dinner of Rwandan banana wine pairing menu.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Bird-watching morning + photography walk
itinerary_7_nights.description: Pre-dawn bird walk with the resident ornithologist — 300+ species in Nyungwe. Late breakfast. Photography walk through the tea fields with the lodge's resident photographer. Evening cinema night under the stars.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Transfer to Volcanoes (or extra day)
itinerary_7_nights.description: Either extend by a day for total decompression (waterfall hike, full spa day) or transfer to Kigali (5h) and onward to Volcanoes for the gorilla portion the next day.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Farewell breakfast and departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Tea-themed breakfast on the terrace with the Gisakura estate's freshest brew. Final canopy photo. Drive to Kigali (5h) for evening departure or extension.

caveats: 5h drive from Kigali — the longest road transfer of any Rwandan luxury lodge.
Chimp trekking is less reliable than gorilla trekking (~70% success rate vs 95%+).
Cool, often misty mountain rainforest climate — pack rain shell + warm layers.
Heated pool is small (10m); not a swim-laps option.
Restaurant menu is somewhat limited; honeymooners often request chef's tasting customisation.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Yes — the rainforest setting, all-suite layout, and slower pace (compared to the gorilla-trek bootcamp) make this the romantic decompression of a Rwanda trip. Couples often book it as the post-gorilla wind-down.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: June–September long dry season for trail access; January–February short dry. Wet seasons make trails muddy but rainforest is more magical.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: 5–6 months ahead. Forest View suites and the master 1 Suite go first.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: No, children 8+ welcome (chimp trek age min 15). Practically adult-leaning.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Forest View Suite for the canopy view; the 1 Suite for honeymoons celebrating something significant.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to Kigali (KGL). 5h drive via Butare to Nyungwe. Charter helicopter from Kigali (~2h, ~$3,500/couple) or scheduled flight to Kamembe airport (45 min) + 1.5h drive.
